claims.claim: Israeli standard VAT rate is 18% in 2026 (raised from 17% on 1 January 2025)
claims.notes: Proposal to raise to 19% in Jan 2026 was rejected; rate remains 18%

claims.claim: Ministry of Finance 2026 budget proposal to raise VAT from 18% to 19% was rejected; rate stays at 18% through 2026
SHAAM allocation number required for B2B tax invoices above NIS 20,000 (pre-VAT) throughout 2025
SHAAM allocation threshold drops to NIS 10,000 (pre-VAT) from 1 January 2026
SHAAM allocation threshold drops further to NIS 5,000 (pre-VAT) from 1 June 2026 (permanent floor)
B2B tax invoices above the active SHAAM threshold without a valid allocation number are not eligible for VAT input deduction by the buyer
Correct formula to extract VAT from a VAT-inclusive amount at 18%: VAT = total * 18 / 118
claims.notes: Math identity, also confirmed in script DECIMAL implementation

claims.claim: Non-commercial vehicle expenses: only 2/3 of input VAT is deductible (1/3 permanently non-deductible)
claims.notes: Long-standing rule under VAT regulations, unchanged for 2026

claims.claim: Entertainment / hosting / meals expenses: only 80% deductible for income tax (irrespective of full VAT deduction)
Osek Patur annual turnover ceiling is NIS 122,833 starting 2026 (raised from NIS 120,000 used in 2024-2025)
Lawyers, doctors, architects, engineers, accountants (and a few others) cannot register as Osek Patur regardless of turnover
Osek Zair (Small Dealer) election allows 30% flat expense deduction for self-employed with annual revenue up to NIS 122,833
Tax invoice (Heshbonit Mas) must be issued within 14 days of the taxable supply or payment, whichever comes first
VAT returns are filed bi-monthly if annual turnover is at or below NIS 1.51M; monthly above that threshold
VAT periodic return deadline is the 15th of the month following the reporting period
Individual income tax annual return (Doch Shnati) deadline: 30 April (paper) or 31 May (online); via מייצג (representative) can extend to November-December
Business records and invoices must be retained for 7 years per Israeli tax law; digital storage is allowed
Israeli business numbers are 9 digits and use a Luhn-like check-digit algorithm
Israeli private company (Hevra Peratit / ח"פ) business numbers begin with 51 or 52
Israeli non-profit association (Amuta) business numbers begin with 58
Israeli invoice document codes: 300 = Tax Invoice, 305 = Tax Invoice/Receipt combined, 310 = Credit Invoice, 320 = Receipt only, 330 = Proforma

Israel uses a clearance model: invoices above threshold are submitted to SHAAM (Israeli Tax Authority Computerized Processing System) via API or web portal, which validates and returns a unique allocation number
Common Israeli depreciation rates: computers 33%, software 33%, office furniture 6%, vehicles 15%, industrial equipment 7-20%
